headline: CATTLE CONVERGE HIGHER, HOGS SPIKE 2%
subheadline: Livestock complex surges as Iran tensions ease and feed costs drop.
lead: Live cattle jumped $242.47, feeders led at $354.52, and hogs spiked 2% to $102.05 in the biggest livestock rally this week. The cattle complex is converging through strength, not breakdown, as Iran-Hormuz tensions ease and corn drops 3 cents to $4.57. Thursday's rally answers the week's question: feeder strength is driving convergence, live cattle weakness isn't.
the_takeaway: Livestock convergence through strength confirms the buyer is patient, not gone.
teaser: Cattle complex surges together as Iran premium fades and feed costs ease.

one_number.unit: oil and gas carriers cleared Hormuz this week
one_number.context: Three oil tankers and two LNG carriers passed through the Strait of Hormuz this week en route to China and India, signaling the shipping lane remains open despite ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. The cleared shipments are easing the energy premium that's been supporting input costs across the livestock sector.

yesterdays_call.summary: Feeders leading confirms convergence through strength, not weakness.
yesterdays_call.note: Feeders led again today at +1.5%, confirming the convergence thesis.

sections.title: Livestock Surges Together
sections.body: Live cattle jumped 1.3% to $242.47, feeders led at +1.5% to $354.52, and hogs spiked 2% to $102.05 in the biggest coordinated livestock rally this week. **Boxed beef prices are sharply higher** and corn dropped 3 cents to $4.57, improving margins across the complex. The cattle convergence is happening through strength, not breakdown. Feeders are leading, live cattle following, and the gap is closing the right way. The overnight hog spike confirms protein demand is real, not just chart noise.
sections.catalyst: Boxed beef prices sharply higher, corn down 3 cents improving margins
sections.bottom_line: Convergence through strength, not weakness, confirms demand structure intact.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: convergence accelerated exactly as feeders continued leading.

sections.title: Grains Ease on Iran Premium Fade
sections.body: Corn closed at $4.57 as **Iran tensions ease** with five oil and gas carriers clearing Hormuz this week. December corn added 2¾ cents to $4.82, the new-crop contract following front-month higher. Soybeans managed a 3¾ cent gain to $11.94 despite meal staying flat at $331.60. Wheat added 3¼ cents to $6.25. The energy premium is fading as shipping lanes stay open, but grains aren't giving back the Iran gains, they're consolidating them. Fund positioning is steady, no liquidation pressure.
sections.bottom_line: Grains holding Iran premium as tensions ease, consolidating not reversing.

sections.title: Energy Premium Unwinds
sections.body: Crude dropped 1.6% to $90.60 as **fresh U.S. strikes on Iran** failed to escalate beyond targeted military sites. Natural gas eased 0.4% to $3.07. The Hormuz shipping data is key: five tankers cleared this week with cargoes heading to China and India, proving the strait remains navigable. Energy markets are pricing less disruption risk, which flows directly into livestock feed costs and fertilizer inputs. The Iran premium isn't gone, but it's shrinking as diplomacy holds the line.
sections.catalyst: Fresh U.S. strikes on Iran remain limited to military targets
sections.bottom_line: Energy premium fading as Hormuz stays open, feeding livestock rally.

outside_the_pit.title: USDA seed bank relocation draws scientific criticism
outside_the_pit.body: The proposed move of two critical national seed banks is drawing fire from university researchers who say relocating genetic resources could disrupt decades of conservation work. The controversy highlights infrastructure decisions that could affect crop breeding programs for years.

outside_the_pit.title: Farm bankruptcies hit six-year high in April
outside_the_pit.body: April saw 62 Chapter 12 farm bankruptcies, a 130% jump from last year and the highest monthly total since February 2020. The spike suggests financial stress is building in rural America despite higher commodity prices.

outside_the_pit.title: Malaysian gas demand hits records on data center boom
outside_the_pit.body: Malaysia is diverting more domestic gas to power generation as data center electricity consumption and heat waves push demand to historic highs. The world's fifth-largest LNG exporter is keeping more gas at home, tightening global supply.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.commentary: The ratio closed at 0.684, the tightest since March, as feeders outpaced live cattle for the fourth straight session. When this ratio moves toward 0.70, it signals the market expects fed cattle supply to tighten faster than feeder demand can fill the gap.

basis.headline: **Eastern Belt cattle basis firms** as packer demand improves
basis.body: Cattle basis is **firming across the Eastern Belt** as boxed beef strength pulls packer bids higher. Feedlot sellers east of the Mississippi have pricing power they haven't seen in weeks. Western Belt staying softer, consistent with the seasonal pattern, but the gap is narrowing as demand spreads geographically. **Feed savings from cheaper corn** are improving cattle margins from both ends.

weekly_thread.question: Will the cattle complex split resolve through feeder strength or live cattle weakness?
weekly_thread.status_text: Thursday's rally confirms resolution through feeder strength. Convergence is happening through coordinated gains, not live cattle breakdown.

the_more_you_know.title: Why livestock convergence tells the demand story
the_more_you_know.body: Today's coordinated livestock rally, with feeders leading at +1.5% and live cattle following at +1.3%, signals demand-driven convergence rather than supply-driven breakdown. When cattle complexes move together higher, it means the market sees protein demand strengthening across the entire production chain. When they converge through weakness, it's usually oversupply or margin compression forcing liquidation. The direction of convergence matters more than the convergence itself.

watch_list.desc: Cattle on Feed report: under 95% of last year keeps the convergence story alive
Iran diplomatic talks resume: watch crude above $92 if talks stall
USDA Crop Progress: corn planting above 85% removes weather premium entirely

daily_quote.text: Never invest in a business you cannot understand.

source_summary: CBOT, CME, NYMEX, USDA, market reports
